#+vindex: johnson-color-gray-face
#+vindex: johnson-color-brown-face
#+vindex: johnson-color-violet-face
#+vindex: johnson-color-orange-face
DSL dictionaries use =[c]...[/c]= and =[c NAME]...[/c]= tags to apply
color to text.  The module maps DSL color names (case-insensitively) to
a fixed set of customizable Emacs faces.  All color faces define
separate foreground colors for light and dark backgrounds.

The face ~johnson-color-default-face~ is used when no color name is
specified (i.e., a bare =[c]= tag).  It defaults to green (=dark green=
on light backgrounds, =green3= on dark backgrounds).

The face ~johnson-color-green-face~ is used for the DSL color names
=green= and =darkgreen=.

The face ~johnson-color-red-face~ is used for =red=, =darkred=, and
=crimson=.

The face ~johnson-color-blue-face~ is used for =blue=, =darkblue=, and
=steelblue=.

The face ~johnson-color-gray-face~ is used for =gray=, =darkgray=, and
=dimgray=.

The face ~johnson-color-brown-face~ is used for =brown= and
=saddlebrown=.

The face ~johnson-color-violet-face~ is used for =violet=, =purple=,
and =darkviolet=.

The face ~johnson-color-orange-face~ is used for =orange= and
=darkorange=.

Any unrecognized color name falls back to ~johnson-color-default-face~.

** UI faces
:PROPERTIES:
:CUSTOM_ID: h:ui-faces
:END:

#+vindex: johnson-toc-face
#+vindex: johnson-audio-button-face
#+vindex: johnson-reorder-unmatched-face
The face ~johnson-toc-face~ renders table-of-contents entries at the
top of the results buffer.  TOC entries are clickable links that jump
to the corresponding dictionary section.  It inherits from ~link~.

The face ~johnson-audio-button-face~ renders audio playback buttons
(=▶=) in dictionary entries ([[#h:audio-playback][Audio playback]]).  It
inherits from ~link~.

The face ~johnson-reorder-unmatched-face~ highlights dictionaries in
the reorder buffer that could not be matched during a GoldenDict
import ([[#h:importing-goldendict-order][Importing GoldenDict order]]).  It
inherits from ~warning~.

* Tips
:PROPERTIES:
:CUSTOM_ID: h:tips
:END:

** Looking up words in PDF documents
:PROPERTIES:
:CUSTOM_ID: h:pdf-lookup
:END:

If you use =pdf-tools= for reading PDFs, you can look up words by
double-clicking them.  The following snippet defines a minor mode
that binds ~[double-mouse-1]~ to a command that extracts the word
under the mouse cursor and passes it to ~johnson-lookup~:

#+begin_src emacs-lisp
(defun my-pdf-lookup-word-at-mouse (ev)
  "Select word at mouse event EV and look it up with `johnson-lookup'."
  (interactive "@e")
  (let* ((posn (event-start ev))
         (xy (posn-object-x-y posn))
         (size (pdf-view-image-size))
         (page (pdf-view-current-page))
         (x (/ (car xy) (float (car size))))
         (y (/ (cdr xy) (float (cdr size))))
         (word (string-trim (pdf-info-gettext page (list x y x y) 'word))))
    (setq pdf-view-active-region
          (cons page (pdf-info-getselection page (list x y x y) 'word)))
    (pdf-view-display-region pdf-view-active-region)
    (when (string-empty-p word)
      (user-error "No word found at click position"))
    (johnson-lookup word)))

(defvar my-pdf-lookup-mode-map
  (let ((map (make-sparse-keymap)))
    (define-key map [double-mouse-1] 'my-pdf-lookup-word-at-mouse)
    map))

(define-minor-mode my-pdf-lookup-mode
  "Minor mode for looking up words in PDFs by double-clicking."
  :keymap my-pdf-lookup-mode-map)

(add-hook 'pdf-view-mode-hook #'my-pdf-lookup-mode)
#+end_src

* Roadmap
:PROPERTIES:
:CUSTOM_ID: h:roadmap
:ID:       A1B2C3D4-E5F6-7890-ABCD-EF1234567890
:END:

** Version 0.1 --- DSL
:PROPERTIES:
:CUSTOM_ID: h:roadmap-v01
:ID:       A2B3C4D5-E6F7-8901-ABCD-EF2345678901
:END:

- [X] =johnson-dsl.el= backend for =.dsl= files.
- [X] Auto-discovery and indexing of uncompressed DSL dictionaries.
- [X] Encoding auto-detection (UTF-8, UTF-16LE, UTF-16BE).
- [X] Headword alternation (={/}=), optional parts (=(...)=), and
  multi-headword entries.
- [X] Case-insensitive and accent-insensitive search via NFKD
  normalization.
- [X] Dynamic =completing-read= with sqlite-backed prefix matching.
- [X] Full DSL markup rendering with text properties and faces.
- [X] Cross-reference navigation with back/forward history.
- [X] Dictionary groups by language pair, with configurable scope.
- [X] Collapsible dictionary sections in the results buffer.
- [X] Dictionary list buffer (=tabulated-list-mode=).
- [X] Completion-at-point (CAPF) backend.
- [X] Plain-text entry copying.

** Version 0.2 --- StarDict and compressed DSL
:PROPERTIES:
:CUSTOM_ID: h:roadmap-v02
:ID:       B2C3D4E5-F6A7-8901-BCDE-F12345678901
:END:

- [X] =johnson-stardict.el= backend for =.ifo=, =.idx=, =.dict=, and
  =.syn= files.
- [X] Dictzip random-access support for compressed =.dict.dz= and
  =.dsl.dz= files.
- [X] StarDict content types: plain text, HTML, Pango markup, phonetic.
- [X] Include table of contents at the beginning of the buffer. Integrate it with ace-link for quick navigation.
- [X] Allow collapsing all sections, not just individual ones (like org does).
- [X] Display a buffer of all indexed dictionaries and allow the user to reorder them, so that the new order can be saved as the new order in =johnson-dictionary-priorities=.
    - When saving, prompt the user whether they want to set the new value via defcustom, for the current session, or to set it manually in their init file; in the latter case, copy the new value to the kill ring and display instructions on how to use it.
- [X] Provide basic media support (audio pronunciation).
  
** Version 0.3 --- MDict and DSL abbreviations
:PROPERTIES:
:CUSTOM_ID: h:roadmap-v03
:ID:       C3D4E5F6-A7B8-9012-CDEF-123456789012
:END:

- [X] =johnson-mdict.el= backend for =.mdx= and =.mdd= files.
- [X] Support for MDict's HTML+CSS content.
- [X] =_abrv.dsl= abbreviation table support for DSL dictionaries
  (=[p]= tag expansion).

** Version 0.4 --- BGL, DICT, and more
:PROPERTIES:
:CUSTOM_ID: h:roadmap-v04
:ID:       D4E5F6A7-B8C9-0123-DEFA-234567890123
:END:

- [X] =johnson-bgl.el= backend for =.bgl= files, with automatic
  detection via file extension and magic bytes (~0x12 0x34~).
- [X] =johnson-dict.el= DICT protocol client (RFC 2229) for remote
  dictionary servers.
- [X] Inline image support across all format backends (DSL =[s]= tags,
  HTML =<img>= tags in MDict, StarDict, and BGL).
- [X] Wildcard search using =?= (single character) and =*= (multiple
  characters) in lookups.
- [X] Full-text search within dictionary definitions
  (~johnson-search~).
- [X] Eldoc integration via ~johnson-eldoc-mode~.
- [X] Scan-popup mode via ~johnson-scan-mode~ (popup definitions on
  selection or idle).
- [X] Bookmarking entries for later reference.
- [X] Browsable history buffer with timestamps and result counts.
- [X] Auto-detection of non-standard 64-bit =.idx= entry formats in
  StarDict dictionaries whose =.ifo= files omit the
  ~idxoffsetbits=64~ declaration.
- [X] Clearer error message when a StarDict =.idx= file is missing
  (incomplete dictionary distribution).

** Version 0.5 --- EPWING

- [X] =johnson-epwing.el= backend for EPWING (JIS X 4081) and EB
  format electronic dictionaries.
- [X] B-tree search index traversal for headword extraction.
- [X] JIS X 0208 (EUC-JP) and ISO 8859-1 character encoding support.
- [X] Fullwidth-to-ASCII normalization for readable output.
- [X] Text rendering with bold, italic, emphasis, references,
  sub/superscript, indentation, and newlines.
- [X] =johnson-ebzip.el= module for EBZIP decompression (compressed
  =.ebz= files).
- [X] Cross-reference buttons navigating via =johnson-lookup=.

** Version 0.6 (current) --- EPWING improvements and audits

- [X] EBZIP decompression improvements and caching.
- [X] Interpretability audit: improved code readability and intent
  transparency.
- [X] Checkdoc and byte-compile cleanups across all backends.
